LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WILLIAM MALLEN'S AMENDED MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED, FOR GOOD CAUSE SHOWN. DEFENDANT HAS MET HIS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT WERE ENTERED AGAINST DEFENDANT DUE TO MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, SURPRISE, OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 473(B) AND THIS MOTION WAS TIMELY FILED. IN ADDITION, PLAINTIFF'S MEMORANDUM IN OPPOSITION APPEARS TO BE BASED ON ALLEGED FACTS REGARDING SERVICE OF A SUMMONS VIA A DIFFERENT PROCESS SERVER IN 2010, ALL OF WHICH CONFLICTS WITH THE PROOF OF SERVICE FILED BY PLAINTIFF IN THIS ACTION REGARDING SERVICE OF PROCESS BY ANOTHER PROCESS SERVER IN 2014. (SEE PLAINTIFF'S MEMO. IN OPPOSITION TO SET ASIDE JUDGMENT AT PAGE 2, LINES 4-10.) DEFENDANT SHALL FILE A PROPOSED ANSWER WITHIN 10 DAYS AFTER NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; SHERYL L. SAWYER CSR# 5976.(302/MJM)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vaqcate Default And Default Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-17-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT WILLIAM MALLEN's Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment is Off calendar. Duplicate entry. (302)

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-17-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kelli Mallen, Esq. of the Law Office of Kelli Mallen, T:415.845.8752, for defendant and moving party;
Donald Sherrill, Esq. of Hunt & Henriques, T:408.677.5822, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WILLIAM MALLEN's Amended Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment is granted, for good cause shown. Defendant has met his burden of showing that the default and default judgment were entered against Defendant due to mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect pursuant to C.C.P. 473(b) and this motion was timely filed. In addition, Plaintiff's memorandum in opposition appears to be based on alleged facts regarding service of a summons via a different process server in 2010, all of which conflicts with the proof of service filed by plaintiff in this action regarding service of process by another process server in 2014. (See Plaintiff's Memo. In Opposition to Set Aside Judgment at page 2, lines 4-10.) Defendant shall file a proposed Answer within 10 days after notice of entry of this order. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Sheryl L. Sawyer CSR# 5976.
